---
title: "Global HIV Epidemic Dashboard"
author: Elvis Safary
format:
    dashboard:
        embed-resources: true
        orientation: rows
theme: flatly
execute:
    echo: false
    warning: false
---

<style>
.value-box-container {border-radius: 10px;
box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.1);
transition: transform 0.3s ease, box-shadow 0.3s ease;}

.value-box-container:hover {transform: translateY(-4px);box-shadow: 0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.15);}

.value-box-value {font-size: 1.8rem !important;font-weight: 700 !important;
    letter-spacing: -0.5px;}

.value-box-title {font-size: 0.85rem !important;font-weight: 600 !important;
color: #6b7280; text-transform: uppercase;
    letter-spacing: 0.5px;}

.dashboard-title {font-size: 2.5rem;   font-weight: 700;margin-bottom: 1rem;    background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);   -webkit-background-clip: text;    -webkit-text-fill-color: transparent;
    background-clip: text;}

.section-header {font-size: 1.5rem;     font-weight: 600;border-left: 4px solid #667eea;padding-left: 12px;
    margin-bottom: 1.5rem;}
</style>


```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import plotly.express as px
from itables import show
import itables.options as opt


#load datasets
combo_data = pd.read_csv('newly_hiv_infected_number_all_ages.csv')
plhiv_raw = pd.read_csv('people_living_with_hiv_number_all_ages.csv')

#data cleaning function
def clean_hiv_data(combo_data, value_name):
    data_long = combo_data.melt(id_vars=['country'], var_name='year', value_name=value_name)
    data_long['year'] = pd.to_numeric(data_long['year'], errors='coerce')     #melt from wide to long (Years as columns to a single column)
    
    #handle numeric formatting (k, M, commas)
    def clean_val(val):
        if pd.isna(val) or val == '': 
            return np.nan
        val = str(val).replace(',', '').strip()
        if 'M' in val: 
            return float(val.replace('M', '')) * 1_000_000
        if 'k' in val: 
            return float(val.replace('k', '')) * 1_000
        try: 
            return float(val)
        except: 
            return np.nan
    
    #apply the cleaning function
    data_long[value_name] = data_long[value_name].apply(clean_val)
    return data_long  # This is correctly indented  now

#clean and merge
new_inf = clean_hiv_data(combo_data, 'new_infections')

plhiv = clean_hiv_data(plhiv_raw, 'plhiv')

hiv_data = pd.merge(new_inf, plhiv, on=['country', 'year'], how='inner')

#assign regions
region_mapping = {'Kenya': 'Africa', 'Tanzania': 'Africa', 'Uganda': 'Africa', 'South Africa': 'Africa',    'Thailand': 'Asia', 'India': 'Asia', 'China': 'Asia', 'Ukraine': 'Europe', 'Russia': 'Europe', 'USA': 'Americas', 'Brazil': 'Americas'}

hiv_data['region'] = hiv_data['country'].map(region_mapping).fillna('Global Others')

#derived metrics
hiv_data['infection_ratio'] = (hiv_data['new_infections'] / hiv_data['plhiv']) * 100

latest_year = int(hiv_data[hiv_data['plhiv'].notna()]['year'].max())

latest_df = hiv_data[hiv_data['year'] == latest_year].copy()

#KPI calculations
total_plhiv = latest_df['plhiv'].sum()
total_new = latest_df['new_infections'].sum()
global_ratio = (total_new / total_plhiv) * 100
total_countries = hiv_data['country'].nunique()

#calculate change since 2010
total_new_2010 = hiv_data[hiv_data['year'] == 2010]['new_infections'].sum()

pct_change_2010 = ((total_new - total_new_2010) / total_new_2010) * 100

#visualization
#| title: "Global Prevalence Heatmap (PLHIV)"
fig_map = px.choropleth(latest_df, locations="country", locationmode='country names', color="plhiv", hover_name="country",    color_continuous_scale=px.colors.sequential.Reds,
title=f"People Living with HIV by Country ({latest_year})")

fig_map.update_layout(margin=dict(l=0, r=0, t=40, b=0))

#| title: "Top 10 Infection Ratios"
# Higher ratio indicates faster epidemic growth relative to treatment base
top_10 = latest_df.nlargest(10, 'infection_ratio')
fig_bar = px.bar(top_10, x='infection_ratio', y='country', orientation='h',   color='infection_ratio', color_continuous_scale='Reds', labels={'infection_ratio': 'Ratio (New/PLHIV %)'}).update_layout(yaxis={'categoryorder':'total ascending'}, showlegend=False)

#| title: "Global Trends: New Infections vs total PLHIV (2000-2011)"
global_trend = hiv_data.groupby('year')[['new_infections', 'plhiv']].sum().reset_index()
fig_line = px.line(global_trend, x='year', y=['new_infections', 'plhiv'], labels={'value': 'Count', 'variable': 'Metric'},color_discrete_map={'new_infections': '#e74c3c', 'plhiv': '#2980b9'},
markers=True)
fig_line.update_layout(showlegend=False,legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1))

#| title: "New Infections vs PLHIV Capacity"
# Create a copy and handle NaN values
latest_df_clean = latest_df.copy()

# Replace NaN infection_ratio with a small default value (e.g., 0.1)
latest_df_clean['infection_ratio'] = latest_df_clean['infection_ratio'].fillna(0.1)

# Filter out zeros for log scale
latest_df_clean = latest_df_clean[(latest_df_clean['plhiv'] > 0) & (latest_df_clean['new_infections'] > 0)]

fig_scatter = px.scatter(latest_df_clean,   x="plhiv", y="new_infections", size="infection_ratio", color="region",    hover_name="country", log_x=True, log_y=True,    title="Scale of Epidemic vs. New Case Burden (Log Scale)", labels={"plhiv": "Total PLHIV", "new_infections": "New Infections"})

```


# Overview
## Row {height="20%"}

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "People Living with HIV"
dict(icon="person-plus", color="primary", value=f"{total_plhiv/1e6:.1f}M")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "New HIV Infections"
dict(icon="graph-down", color="danger", value=f"{total_new/1e6:.2f}M")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Global Infection Ratio"
dict(icon="globe", color="info", value=f"{global_ratio:.1f}%")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "% Change (Since 2010)"
dict(icon="activity", color="warning", value=f"{pct_change_2010:+.1f}%")
```

## Row {height="80%"}
### {width="50%"}

```{python}
fig_map
```

### {width="50%"}

```{python}
fig_bar
```

# Epidemic Analysis
## Row {height="20%"}
```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Countries Tracked"
dict(icon="globe-americas", color="info", value=f"{total_countries}")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Latest Data Year"
dict(icon="calendar-check", color="teal", value=f"{latest_year}")
```

## Row {height="80%"}
### Column {width="50%"}

```{python}
fig_line
```

### Column {width="50%"}

```{python}
fig_scatter
```

# Data Source and Summary

## Summary of Findings {height="40%"}

Epidemic Control: The Global Infection Ratio of 6.3% suggests a slowing trend, but regional disparities remain stark.

Top Performer: Asia showed the most significant decrease in new infections (-72%) between 2000 and 2011.

Critical Gaps: Countries with high infection ratios (seen in the bar chart) represent areas where the epidemic is outpacing current interventions.

Log Scale Insights: The scatter plot shows that even countries with massive PLHIV populations are successfully reducing new case counts, moving toward the 95-95-95 targets.

## Row {height="60%"}

```{python}
opt.classes = ["display", "nowrap", "compact"]
show(hiv_data[['country', 'year', 'new_infections', 'plhiv', 'region', 'infection_ratio']], paging=True, buttons=['csv', 'excel'])
```
